channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US airstrikes targeting a Yemeni oil port killed 20 people, Houthis say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. airstrikes targeting the Ras Isa oil port held by Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed 20 people and wounded 50 others, the group said early Friday. The strikes, confirmed by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fear and panic at Florida State as deadly shooting sends students fleeing
item.description: When a 20-year-old opened fire at Florida State University, terrified students barricaded doors and fled across campus, abandoning chemistry notes and even shoes.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And David Fischer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration seeks explosive expansion of nation's immigration detention system
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Amid rural Louisiana's crawfish farms, towering pine trees and cafes serving po'boys, nearly 7,000 people are waiting at immigration detention centers to learn whether they will be expelled from the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: German doctor indicted in 15 murders, suspected in 95: report
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Ralph Lauren stays closer to home this time with intimate Manhattan gallery show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ralph Lauren, known for staging elaborate runway shows in sumptuous settings like the horsey Hamptons or amid his vintage car collection, took it down a notch for a more intimate show Thursday in a Manhattan gallery space.
item.dc:creator: BROOKE LEFFERTS and JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Federal judge in Baltimore temporarily limits DOGE access to Social Security data
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday imposed new restrictions on billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, limiting its access to Social Security systems that hold personal data on millions of Americans. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Carney says lower internal trade barriers will help Canada more than Trump's tariffs will harm it
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday eliminating trade barriers within Canada would benefit Canadians far more than U.S. President Donald Trump can ever take away with his trade war as he made his case to retain power at the last debate ahead of the April 28 vote.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: A shooting on the Florida State University campus left multiple victims Thursday, and police took a suspect into custody, a person familiar with the matter told The Associated Press. The university issued an active shooter alert at midday near the student union.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York State Republican Party headquarters vandalised with swastikas
item.description: The swastikas were apparently printed on standard letter-sized paper and duct-taped to the front window, the front door, and the building’s front sign.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Actor Haley Joel Osment booked for 'public intoxication' at California ski resort
item.description: Actor Haley Joel Osment, famous for his role as the child who saw “dead people” in the 1999 thriller “The Sixth Sense” starring Bruce Willis, was reportedly arrested at a ski resort in Mammoth Lakes for public intoxication, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: Over 150 dogs rescued from 'extremely poor' conditions inside home
item.description: 152 dogs were rescued from a home in Ogden, Utah, where they were reportedly living in "extremely poor and overcrowded" conditions, according to the Ogden Police Department. Lt.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, Fox 5

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration issues order to stop construction on New York offshore wind project
item.description: The Trump administration issued an order Wednesday to stop construction on a major offshore wind project to power more than 500,000 New York homes, the latest in a series of moves targeting the industry.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-NYPD officer sentenced to 18 months after conviction for helping China stalk an expat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former New York police sergeant was sentenced Wednesday to 18 months in prison in a U.S. case about China's pursuit of critics abroad, a sentence that came after two members of Congress urged the judge to spare him from time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman sues McAlister's Deli, claims tooth found in meal
item.description: A Myrtle Beach restaurant is being sued for negligence after a woman claims she cracked her molar on a tooth that was in her meal.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zoom outage impacting users nationwide, service restored according to update
item.description: (KRON) -- Teleconferencing software platform experienced a nationwide outage Wednesday. According to reports on Downdetector.com, users cited problems with the platform's website and app, along with log-in issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, Fox 5

item.title: Conservation group names Mississippi River 'most endangered,' cites proposed FEMA cuts
item.description: American Rivers, a nonprofit environmental advocacy group, said the Mississippi River in recent years has faced “increasingly frequent and severe floods” that have damaged homes and businesses and worsened the health of the river, which provides drinking water for 20 million people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the host of game shows who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died at 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wink Martindale, the genial game show host and an early TV interviewer of Elvis Presley, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, ABC 4
